subject: Chemistry
title: Photosynthesis: Sunlight to Sugar
description: A 60-second lesson on Photosynthesis: Sunlight to Sugar.

hook.text: Every leaf is a tiny chemical factory turning sunlight, water, and CO₂ into sugar and oxygen.

buildup.text: Chlorophyll captures photons. The energy splits water molecules, releasing oxygen as a waste product.

discovery.text: The captured energy then builds glucose from CO₂—six carbon atoms linked into a sugar ring.

twist.text: All the oxygen you breathe is 'waste' from plants. Photosynthesis created Earth's breathable atmosphere.

climax.text: Fossil fuels are ancient photosynthesis—sunlight stored by plants millions of years ago.

punchline.text: Light in, sugar out, oxygen as waste. The reaction that feeds everything.

quiz.question: Where does the oxygen we breathe come from in photosynthesis?
options: From water molecules split by light energy
From carbon dioxide being broken apart
